items.headline: AI Turns Coders, Lawyers and Analysts Into Ghosts of London’s Past
items.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Search for “finance analyst” and you’re likely to find around 80 vacancies in London, according to a prominent recruitment website. If that seems like a surprisingly low number for one of the world’s biggest banking and corporate hubs, it’s because it is — four years earlier, the same website featured more than 350 of these roles.Most Read from BloombergWhy Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPOTrump Eyes Sunday Iran Deal But Tehran Says Still Reviewing TextUS

items.headline: Accenture Expands Creator Marketing With Whalar Acquisition As Shares Trade Lower
items.summary: Accenture, NYSE:ACN, has agreed to acquire creator marketing firm Whalar to expand its social commerce and influencer marketing capabilities. The deal will bring Whalar’s leadership and more than 170 employees into Accenture Song. The acquisition is intended to deepen Accenture’s digital marketing solutions and creator focused services for clients. Accenture’s move to buy Whalar comes at a time when NYSE:ACN is trading at $170.28, with the stock down 34.5% year to date and down 43.9% over...

items.summary: Accenture (NYSE:ACN) is one of the most undervalued quality stocks to invest in.
